entries.final_answer: operator_required: require a live /api/health self-report for safe_auto; a wedged/unresponsive in-range listener with cmdline scope-proof is operator_required (surfaced, cleaned by --reset, never auto-killed at startup).
options: operator_required (require a live health self-report for safe_auto)
entries.question: How should a sync-range listener that does NOT answer /api/health (wedged/unresponsive) but has provable cmdline scope markers be classified?

entries.final_answer: Guard operator_required behind explicit confirmation: --reset auto-cleans safe_auto, but requires interactive y/N (or --force in --json/non-interactive) before killing operator_required (cross-root/different-HOME/ambiguous) daemons.
options: Require explicit --force or confirmation before killing operator_required
--reset cleans all cleanable (safe_auto + operator_required) in one shot
entries.question: How should 'auth doctor --reset' treat operator_required daemons (cross-root / pre-marker / ambiguous)?

entries.final_answer: Automated regression test in the live-subprocess harness reproducing the same-HOME singleton scenario; close #1071 with durable test evidence.
options: Automated regression test in the live-subprocess harness
Manual reconfirmation plus documentation in the ADR/PR
entries.question: How should issue #1071 (same-$HOME singleton leak) be live-reconfirmed for FR-012/SC-006?
